[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: Allies and Morrison - Pitch perfect | Allies and Morrison
[METADATA/INTRO]: Highbury Square View all projectsPitch perfect...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: The existing four-metre structural grid of the stands was used as a planning grid for the whole project, including the landscaped gardens that occupy the space where the pitch once was. A shared garden, the relationship between the buildings and public realm has much in common with the Georgian London square, where a group of architecturally similar buildings frame a formal garden, albeit in the case super-sized. 
 Apartments are inserted into the structure of the east and west stands, with a focus and orientation towards the garden through extensive glazing and balconies. 
 On the rest of the site additional infill residential accommodation around a series of smaller courtyards creates a high-density residential development. In total Highbury Square provides over 25 different housing typologies including interlocking duplexes, triplexes and penthouses, across a mix of tenures.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: Green Roofs: a Key Ingredient to Happier & Healthier Cities - NAK
[METADATA/INTRO]: Urban populations are growing, and the challenge of creating a healthy and sustainable urban ecosystem is more important than ever. Green roofs, which are specially designed landscapes on top of commercial and residential buildings, can help regulate a building’s internal...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Urban populations are growing, and the challenge of creating a healthy and sustainable urban ecosystem is more important than ever. 
 Green roofs, which are specially designed landscapes on top of commercial and residential buildings, can help regulate a building’s internal temperature, improve air quality, reduce stormwater run-off, and reduce the urban “heat island” effect – all while improving quality of life for residents by providing new public gathering spaces to reconnect with nature. 
 NAK Design Strategies’ Landscape Architecture and Urban Design staff have a wealth of experience in green roof design and construction, thanks in no small part to the forward-thinking policies of its home city.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: Pride of the Gunners: High praise for Highbury Square | London Evening Standard | The Standard
[METADATA/INTRO]: Pride of the Gunners: High praise for Highbury Square Most of us know that football is a power in the land....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Turn right and you find yourself passing through the towers and streets of new housing built by the club, crossing some railway tracks by a specially built bridge, and encircling the imposing if slightly tinny-looking new Emirates Stadium. 
 Turn left and you come to a newly completed garden square, with 725 homes around it, created by Arsenal out of the old stadium. 
 This square is of a kind that planners and architects are forever praising, and which most people probably quite like too, but which is rarely achieved in London. 
 Its models are the high, stuccoed blocks of Maida Vale, with lush shared gardens in their heart, or the green centre of Dolphin Square in Pimlico. Like them, Highbury Square shows how you can fit a lot of homes on an urban site without feeling crowded.
